title: Credential Compromise: Excessive Address Diversity in 7-Day Rolling Window
narrative: A stolen card credential is systematically tested across multiple shipping addresses within a week, appearing as legitimate one-off purchases individually but revealing fraud through aggregate address diversity. The fraudster uses the compromised card details with different delivery addresses each time, possibly cycling through drop addresses or testing card limits. Each transaction looks normal in isolation—amounts are typical, email and billing info may match cardholder records—but the card's 7-day aggregation shows an abnormal pattern of distinct shipping addresses that exceeds normal cardholder behavior. This pattern exploits the fact that individual transaction checks pass while the rolling-window aggregation exposes the coordinated abuse. The card-counter features reveal what behavioral analysis misses: a single card being leveraged across too many distinct delivery contexts in too short a timeframe.
indicators: C8 > 29 (p90), indicating card used with excessive distinct addresses in 7-day window
C13 > 24 (p90), elevated count of card-address pairings in rolling window
card1_distinct_addr1 >= 8 across recent transactions within dataset timeframe
C1 > 60 (p90), total card transaction counter elevated suggesting high utilization
C14 <= 7 (p90 threshold), device count remains modest per card
TransactionAmt between 50-200, within typical purchase range avoiding fraud triggers
indicator_explanations: Card has been shipped to an unusually high number of different addresses recently
The same card keeps appearing with new shipping address combinations
Analysis shows eight or more distinct delivery addresses for this single card
Overall card usage frequency is elevated beyond normal cardholder patterns
Device variety stays low suggesting same fraudster using the stolen credential
Purchase amounts appear normal and consistent with legitimate shopping behavior
summary: A compromised card is used across many different shipping addresses within a week, with each transaction appearing normal individually but the 7-day address diversity revealing systematic credential abuse.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders rarely ship to more than 2-3 addresses per week; elevated C8 and C13 counters combined with high distinct address counts indicate a fraudster cycling through drop locations or testing the stolen credential's limits while keeping per-transaction behavior clean.
